name: Track job applicants with Google Sheets and Microsoft Teams

parameters.formTitle: Track job applicants with Google Sheets and Microsoft Teams

parameters.message: =Thanks for your interest — here's a quick-start guide.

parameters.content:
### New Candidate Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Role, Stage). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Score the Lead

### Score the Lead

Custom JavaScript: Score each candidate 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Assign Round-Robin Owner

### Assign Round-Robin Owner

Custom JavaScript: Round-robin owner assignment

Next: Route by Heat

### Route by Heat

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: hot, warm + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping · ②→ Warm → Drip via Microsoft Teams · ③→ Cold → Park in Nurture List

### Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping

Upserts a row into 'Candidate Tracker', matched on `candidateId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Next: Notify Assigned Owner

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping

parameters.content:
### Notify Assigned Owner

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Next: Combine Lead Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Warm → Drip via Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Next: Combine Lead Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Warm → Drip via Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Cold → Park in Nurture List

Appends a new row to 'Candidate Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Next: Combine Lead Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Cold → Park in Nurture List

parameters.content:
### Combine Lead Paths

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

# Track job applicants with Google Sheets and Microsoft Teams

**Pattern:** Recruitment Pipeline · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Track job applicants with Google Sheets and Microsoft Teams. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual screening is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Candidate Form Submission → Score the Lead → Assign Round-Robin Owner → Route by Heat → Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping → Notify Assigned Owner → Combine Lead Paths

## Use it for
- Teams that want to recruitment pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating screening in human resources
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Round-robin owner assignment happens right after scoring, before routing — every lead gets an accountable owner regardless of tier, so even a 'cold' lead parked in the nurture list is someone's responsibility later, not an anonymous row in a spreadsheet.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Change the hot/warm threshold values and watch the tier distribution shift.
2. Add a 4th owner to the round-robin pool.
3. Add a follow-up wait+check to the warm-drip branch, similar to the drip-ladder spine.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your screening process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
